assertions.description: A normal same-origin cookie can still be set and read by document.cookie.
A __Http- cookie cannot be minted from script because script cannot set the required HttpOnly attribute.
assertions.test: (()=>{const n='__Http-cps_'+Math.random().toString(36).slice(2);document.cookie=n+'=1; Secure; Path=/; SameSite=Lax';const ok=!document.cookie.includes(n+'=1');document.cookie=n+'=; Max-Age=0; Secure; Path=/';return ok;})()

assertions.description: A __Host-Http- cookie is rejected from script because it also requires HttpOnly.
assertions.test: (()=>{const n='__Host-Http-cps_'+Math.random().toString(36).slice(2);document.cookie=n+'=1; Secure; Path=/; SameSite=Lax';const ok=!document.cookie.includes(n+'=1');document.cookie=n+'=; Max-Age=0; Secure; Path=/';return ok;})()

assertions.description: A __Http- cookie without Secure is rejected even before considering HttpOnly.
Cookie Store API writes cannot mint __Http- cookies without HttpOnly semantics.
assertions.test: (async()=>{if(!('cookieStore' in window)||typeof cookieStore.set!=='function')return true;const n='__Http-cps_store_'+Math.random().toString(36).slice(2);try{await cookieStore.set({name:n,value:'1',secure:true,path:'/'});const c=await cookieStore.get(n);if(c)await cookieStore.delete(n);return !c;}catch(e){return true;}})()
